title: Understand: the rock cycle
description: Check your understanding of the rock cycle in this set of free practice questions aligned to NGSS standards.
topic_path: Middle school Earth and space science - NGSS

assessment_items.question_content:
Quartzite is a hard rock that has been used since prehistoric times to make stone tools. It forms when sandstone is exposed to intense heat and pressure.

**What type of rock is quartzite?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: igneous
sedimentary
metamorphic

assessment_items.question_content:
Shale is a rock that forms when clay and silt particles are compacted and cemented. It is composed of many thin, parallel layers, as shown in the image below.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What type of rock is shale?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.alt: Thin rock layers in a shale outcrop.

choices.content: igneous
sedimentary
metamorphic

assessment_items.question_content:
Pumice is a highly porous volcanic rock. It forms when lava containing a large amount of dissolved gas is ejected from a volcano. As the lava cools and hardens, the gas escapes, forming bubbles that are preserved in the solid rock. An image of pumice is shown below.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What type of rock is pumice?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.alt: A pumice stone with many pores, or holes, on its surface.

choices.content: igneous
sedimentary
metamorphic
through the cooling and hardening of magma or lava
through the compaction and cementation of sediment
through the weathering and erosion of existing rocks
through the lithification of small pieces of rocks and shells
through the alteration of existing rocks by heat and pressure
through the solidification of lava or magma
through the heating and squeezing of existing rocks
through the compaction and cementation of sediment
through the cooling and solidification of lava or magma

assessment_items.question_content:
During the rock cycle, sediment is created when wind or water breaks down existing rocks.

**Which process does this best describe?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: weathering
erosion
deposition

assessment_items.question_content:
During the rock cycle, sediment is transported by natural forces from one place to another.

**Which process does this best describe?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: weathering
erosion
deposition

assessment_items.question_content:
During the rock cycle, sediment carried by wind or water is dropped in a new location.

**Which process does this best describe?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: weathering
erosion
deposition

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statement correctly describes the difference between magma and lava?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Magma exists beneath Earth’s surface, while lava exists on top of Earth’s surface.
Magma is made up of solid rock, while lava is made up of liquid rock.
Magma is an organic material, while lava is an inorganic material.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statement correctly describes the difference between lava and magma?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Lava is found above Earth’s surface, while magma is found below Earth’s surface.
Lava is an organic substance, while magma is an inorganic substance.
Lava is composed of molten rock, while magma is composed of solid rock.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statement correctly describes the difference between magma and lava?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Magma is located in Earth’s interior, while lava is located on Earth’s surface.
Magma is composed of inorganic matter, while lava is composed of organic matter.
Magma exists in the solid state, while lava exists in the molten or liquid state.
